what: A Computational Pipeline for Entropy-Informed Detection of Emerging Viral Variants
title: A Computational Pipeline for Entropy-Informed Detection of Emerging Viral Variants
text: ViralEntropR R package efficient preprocessing large FASTA archives computational surveillance emerging viral variants. provides fully vectorized preprocessing layer, including header parsing, country date extraction, ambiguity filtering, amino-acid encoding, together core computational components downstream analysis: per-site Shannon entropy (Shannon 1948), time-window partitioning, Gaussian-mixture-based entropy clustering (Everitt et al. 2011; Scrucca et al. 2016), pairwise Hellinger distances (van der Vaart 1998) empirical amino-acid distributions, non-parametric change-point detection (Fryzlewicz 2014; Matteson James 2014). controlled variant-simulation engine included benchmarking detection performance known ground truth. package developed alongside proposed analysis pipeline emerging-variant detection, demonstrated bundled vignettes: GMM-driven selection high-entropy sites, partitioning around medoids Gower distance (Gower 1971; Kaufman Rousseeuw 1990; Rousseeuw 1987) selected sites, t-SNE visualization cluster geometry (van der Maaten Hinton 2008), non-parametric change-point detection validation, hierarchical agglomerative clustering (HAC) (Everitt et al. 2011; Murtagh Legendre 2014; Sangalli et al. 2010; Tucker et al. 2013) per-site entropy curves time-indexed Hellinger-distance trajectories, treated within functional-data-analysis framework (Ramsay Silverman 2005) discrete realizations continuous functions time. pipeline evaluated filtered, post-processed SARS-CoV-2 Spike-protein sequences: 137,132 raw NCBI records (Sayers et al. 2022) reduced 109,536 US-filtered sequences (archived Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19040165), 16.7 million raw GISAID records (Shu McCauley 2017) reduced 129,371 unique US-filtered sequences. methods general enough apply aligned amino-acid nucleotide time series.

title: A Computational Pipeline for Entropy-Informed Detection of Emerging Viral Variants
text: package ships 100-sequence sample NCBI SARS-CoV-2 Spike sequences inst/extdata/ runnable examples: full pipeline — including time-window partitioning, Hellinger distances, change-point detection, visualization — see bundled vignettes.
code: # This example requires Biostrings (Bioconductor): # install.packages("BiocManager"); BiocManager::install("Biostrings")  library(ViralEntropR)  # 1. Load the bundled sample (100 NCBI Spike sequences). sample_path <- system.file("extdata", "sarscov2_sample.fasta.gz",                            package = "ViralEntropR") fasta <- Biostrings::readAAStringSet(sample_path)  # 2. Extract dates and countries from FASTA headers. #    NCBI Virus format: option = 4 (date at end), position = 2 (between pipes). dates_result     <- extract_fasta_dates(fasta, option = 4) countries_result <- extract_fasta_countries(fasta, position = 2) # diagnostic: were any headers unparseable? dates_result$message        countries_result$message  # 3. Drop sequences whose date or country could not be parsed. #    `missing_id` is NA when nothing failed and an integer vector of indices #    otherwise — strip NAs from the union before use. ids      <- c(dates_result$missing_id, countries_result$missing_id) drop_ids <- unique(ids[!is.na(ids)]) keep     <- setdiff(seq_len(length(fasta)), drop_ids)  fasta           <- fasta[keep] corrected_dates <- dates_result$corrected_dates[keep] countries       <- countries_result$countries[keep]  # 4. Convert to a character matrix, drop sequences with ambiguous residues #    (B, J, X, Z), and align metadata to the surviving rows. char_mat        <- fasta_to_char_matrix(fasta) filtered        <- filter_ambiguous_sequences(char_mat, option = 2) keep_idx        <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(char_mat)), filtered$DeletedSeqId) corrected_dates <- corrected_dates[keep_idx] countries       <- countries[keep_idx]  # 5. Integer-encode under the 25-symbol ViralEntropR alphabet. int_mat <- encode_aa_sequence(filtered$FilteredMatrix)  # 6. Assemble the analysis-ready data frame: sites 1..1273 + Date + Country. AL_df           <- as.data.frame(int_mat) colnames(AL_df) <- as.character(seq_len(ncol(int_mat))) AL_df$Date      <- as.Date(format(corrected_dates, "%Y-%m-01")) AL_df$Country   <- countries AL_df           <- AL_df[order(AL_df$Date), ]  # 7. Per-site Shannon entropy + GMM-based site classification. #    Sites of zero entropy and singletons are excluded.  ent         <- apply(int_mat, 2, calculate_entropy) cls         <- cluster_sites_by_entropy(ent, nr = nrow(int_mat)) cls_labeled <- relabel_entropy_classes(cls$DataFrame) head(cls_labeled, 10)

title: ViralEntropR: A Computational Pipeline for Entropy-Informed Detection of Emerging Viral Variants — ViralEntropR-package
text: package supports four-stage workflow: Preprocessing. Parse FASTA headers, filter ambiguous residues, convert integer character representations amino acid sequences 25-symbol alphabet. See extract_fasta_dates, extract_fasta_countries, fasta_to_char_matrix, filter_ambiguous_sequences, encode_aa_sequence, decode_aa_sequence. Site selection. Compute per-site Shannon entropy across temporal partitions cluster sites entropy via Gaussian mixture models. See calculate_entropy, partition_time_windows, cluster_sites_by_entropy, relabel_entropy_classes. Distributional analysis. Quantify residue-composition shifts time windows using Hellinger distance. See calculate_hellinger_matrix. Change-point detection. Identify temporal change points non-parametrically using energy statistics wild binary segmentation. See detect_changepoints_ecp detect_changepoints_hdcp.

title: Calculate Hellinger Distance Matrix — calculate_hellinger_matrix
text: partitions list data frames, one per time window. data frame must numeric-encoded amino acid sequences columns (integers 1 aa_levels). typically $Partitions element returned partition_time_windows. sites Integer vector. Indices sites analyse. Defaults sites (seq_len(ncol(partitions[[1]]))). aa_levels Integer. Alphabet size. Must match encoding used partitions created: encode_aa_sequence produces values 1–25 default (20 standard residues, three ambiguous codes, *, -). Default 25L. normalized Logical. TRUE, scales distances \(1/\sqrt{2}\) bound result \([0, 1]\). Default FALSE. labels Character vector partition labels. Length must equal length(partitions). first label names reference partition; subsequent labels become column names returned matrix. Defaults "T1", "T2", … include_freq_tables Logical. title: Calculate Hellinger Distance Matrix — calculate_hellinger_matrix
text: include_freq_tables = FALSE (default): numeric matrix rows corresponding sites columns corresponding labels[-1], entry Hellinger distance reference partition (labels[1]) site. Row names site indices; column names taken labels[-1]. default labels, reference partition "T1" matrix columns "T2", "T3", …. include_freq_tables = TRUE: named list elements Sites, Hellinger_Distances, Frequency_Tables, Proportions_Tables.

title: Calculate Hellinger Distance Matrix — calculate_hellinger_matrix
text: Hellinger distance two discrete distributions \(P\) \(Q\) : $$H(P, Q) = \sqrt{\sum_{=1}^{k} (\sqrt{p_i} - \sqrt{q_i})^2}$$ normalized = TRUE result scaled \(1/\sqrt{2}\), bounding distance \([0, 1]\). Otherwise range \([0, \sqrt{2}]\). Internally, amino acid counts per site per partition tabulated using get_site_counts (built tabulate). Per-partition proportions Hellinger distances computed fully vectorised matrix operations — inner loop partitions.

title: Calculate Hellinger Distance Matrix — calculate_hellinger_matrix
code: # Toy 3-partition data: site 1 starts homogeneous (all Alanine, 1), # acquires Valine (20) across two later partitions; site 2 stays # constant (all Valine throughout). p1 = data.frame(s1 = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1),  s2 = c(20, 20, 20, 20, 20)) p2 = data.frame(s1 = c(20, 20, 20, 20, 20), s2 = c(20, 20, 20, 20, 20)) p3 = data.frame(s1 = c(1, 1, 20, 20, 20), s2 = c(20, 20, 20, 20, 20)) parts = list(T1 = p1, T2 = p2, T3 = p3)  result = calculate_hellinger_matrix(parts, sites = 1:2) print(result) #>         T2        T3 #> 1 1.414214 0.8573733 #> 2 0.000000 0.0000000 # Site 1 has nonzero distance in T2 and T3 (composition shift). # Site 2 has zero distance throughout (constant).  # With raw frequency tables for inspection. result2 = calculate_hellinger_matrix(parts, sites = 1:2,                                       include_freq_tables = TRUE) print(result2$Frequency_Tables[[1]]) #>       T1 T2 T3 #>  [1,]  5  0  2 #>  [2,]  0  0  0 #>  [3,]  0  0  0 #>  [4,]  0  0  0 #>  [5,]  0  0  0 #>  [6,]  0  0  0 #>  [7,]  0  0  0 #>  [8,]  0  0  0 #>  [9,]  0  0  0 #> [10,]  0  0  0 #> [11,]  0  0  0 #> [12,]  0  0  0 #> [13,]  0  0  0 #> [14,]  0  0  0 #> [15,]  0  0  0 #> [16,]  0  0  0 #> [17,]  0  0  0 #> [18,]  0  0  0 #> [19,]  0  0  0 #> [20,]  0  5  3 #> [21,]  0  0  0 #> [22,]  0  0  0 #> [23,]  0  0  0 #> [24,]  0  0  0 #> [25,]  0  0  0

text: entropies Numeric vector cluster. package's primary use case per-site Shannon entropy values, univariate numeric vector accepted. nr Integer. Total number sequences entropies computed. Required removesngl = TRUE. nsites Integer. Expected number sites. mismatches length(entropies), actual length used warning. Default length(entropies). precision Integer. Decimal places rounding singleton threshold comparison -identical uniqueness check. Default 6. removez Logical. TRUE, removes sites entropy = 0 (invariant sites), using small tolerance 1e-9 absorb floating-point near-zeros. Default TRUE. removesngl Logical. TRUE, removes sites whose entropy equals singleton value (one differing sequence nr). Uses tolerance-based comparison. Default TRUE. transfr function, object class transform $transform() method, applied entropies clustering. Default NULL (transformation). verbose Logical. TRUE, emits diagnostic warnings non-fatal events (empty partitions, Mclust failures, etc.). Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed Mclust.

title: Cluster a Univariate Numeric Vector by Gaussian Mixture Model — cluster_sites_by_entropy
text: named list two elements: FitObject raw Mclust result, minimal list(classification = integer(0L)) clustering bypassed failed. DataFrame data frame columns sites (original site indices), entropies (values transformation), class (GMM cluster label). class column always present every return path, including zero-row DataFrames. Downstream consumers need guard nrow(df) > 0 accessing class values. Raw Mclust labels returned -; call relabel_entropy_classes standardise label ordering.

title: Cluster a Univariate Numeric Vector by Gaussian Mixture Model — cluster_sites_by_entropy
text: package's typical use, sites clustered Shannon entropy identify groups residue positions similar variability across sequence collection. Two preprocessing rules apply clustering entropies: removez = TRUE drops invariant sites (entropy = 0), removesngl = TRUE drops singleton sites whose entropy corresponds exactly one differing sequence across nr rows. Class assignment rules (applied priority order): rows remaining filtering: empty DataFrame returned zero-length class column (consistent schema). Single row remaining: class 1 assigned directly; Mclust called (undefined 1 observation). entropies identical: class 999 sites (sentinel — one undifferentiated group). Normal Mclust result: raw class labels 1, 2, ..., G. Mclust's integer labels, ordered increasing component mean (univariate Mclust orders components mean) — call relabel_entropy_classes() returned data frame obtain application-friendly class labels (highest-entropy class = 1, lowest-entropy class = G). relabel_entropy_classes returned DataFrame standardise class 1 = highest-entropy group. Mclust failure: empty DataFrame returned (schema -rows case), treating partition uninformative.

title: Encode Amino Acid Sequences — encode_aa_sequence
text: Encoding single vectorised lookup fixed named map. input matrix flattened, normalised uppercase, indexed alphabet's name vector one operation, reshaped — per-row per-element loop. 25-symbol alphabet covers twenty standard residues (=1, R=2, N=3, D=4, C=5, Q=6, E=7, G=8, H=9, =10, L=11, K=12, M=13, F=14, P=15, S=16, T=17, W=18, Y=19, V=20), three IUPAC ambiguous codes B=21, Z=22, X=23, stop codon *=24, alignment gap -=25. character outside alphabet — including NA, empty string, lowercase letters standard set uppercasing (e.g. J leucine/isoleucine), non-letter symbols — maps sentinel 0. sentinel recognised downstream functions: filter_ambiguous_sequences treats 0 ambiguous (alongside B, X, Z) removes affected sequences, decode_aa_sequence round-trips back string "0". Encode a simple matrix of sequences. seq_mat = matrix(c("A", "R", "N", "D"), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) encoded = encode_aa_sequence(seq_mat) print(encoded) #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    1    2 #> [2,]    3    4  # 2. Gaps and unknown characters. # '-' maps to 25; '?' is not in the alphabet and maps to the sentinel 0. gapped_mat = matrix(c("A", "-", "G", "?"), nrow = 1) encode_aa_sequence(gapped_mat) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] #> [1,]    1   25    8    0  # 3. Lowercase input is accepted (uppercased before lookup). encode_aa_sequence(matrix(c("a", "r", "n", "d"), nrow = 2)) #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    1    3 #> [2,]    2    4  # 4. NA and empty string both map to 0. encode_aa_sequence(matrix(c("A", NA, "G", ""), nrow = 2)) #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    1    8 #> [2,]    0    0

title: Partition Data into Time Windows — partition_time_windows
text: data Data frame. Must contain column named Date coercible Date. Columns 1 n_sites must numeric site columns; additional columns (Country per-sequence metadata) may follow. n_sites Integer. Number site columns. Site columns must occupy positions 1 n_sites data; columns (Date, optionally Country, etc.) must come . window_length Integer. Window duration months. Default 1. window_type Integer (1, 2, 3). Windowing strategy: 1 = Cumulative, 2 = Sliding, 3 = Disjoint. Default 3. start_date Date character. Window start. Defaults earliest date data. end_date Date character. Window end (cutoff). Defaults latest date data. date_format Character. Format string date labels. Default "%b-%Y". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress bar completion summary. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed cluster_sites_by_entropy.

title: Partition Data into Time Windows — partition_time_windows
text: named list: Partitions List data frame chunks, one per window. Entropies List numeric entropy vectors, one per window. Clusters List clustering results cluster_sites_by_entropy, one per window. Max_Entropy Numeric vector. Maximum cluster class label per window (equals number clusters found; NA window empty). Dates_Labels Character vector window label strings. N_partitions Integer. Total number windows.

title: Partition Data into Time Windows — partition_time_windows
text: Three windowing strategies supported via window_type. Throughout descriptions , T denotes number whole months start_date end_date, w denotes window_length: 1 — Cumulative: Start fixed; end expands w months step. window includes prior data plus one period. Produces floor(T / w) chunks. 2 — Sliding: window w months slides one month time. Produces T - w + 1 chunks. 3 — Disjoint: Consecutive non-overlapping windows w months. Produces floor(T / w) chunks. Default. example, T = 12 months w = 2: cumulative produces 6 chunks (progressively larger); sliding produces 11 chunks (overlapping, 2 months wide); disjoint produces 6 chunks (exactly 2 months, non-overlapping). Empty windows. window contains observations, corresponding entries returned lists carry placeholder values: Entropies zero-vector length n_sites; Clusters schema-consistent empty result matching cluster_sites_by_entropy's empty-input return; Max_Entropy NA_integer_. Downstream consumers can guard nrow(Partitions[[]]) > 0 !.na(Max_Entropy[]). Column layout requirement. function extracts site columns data[, seq_len(n_sites), drop = FALSE]. site columns must therefore occupy positions 1 n_sites. Date (metadata columns Country) must come site columns. common error place Date first; produce incorrect entropy values.

code: dates <- seq(as.Date("2020-01-01"), as.Date("2020-06-01"), by = "month") df <- data.frame(   s1   = 1L,   s2   = c(rep(1L, 15), rep(2L, 15)),   Date = rep(dates, each = 5) ) res <- partition_time_windows(df, n_sites = 2, window_length = 2,                               window_type = 3, verbose = TRUE) #> Partitioning 2 disjoint windows ... #>    |                                                           |                                                  |   0%   |                                                           |=========================                         |  50%   |                                                           |==================================================| 100% #> Partitioning complete: 2 partitions generated (Jan-2020 to Jun-2020). print(res$Dates_Labels) #> [1] "Jan-2020 - Feb-2020" "Mar-2020 - Apr-2020" print(res$Max_Entropy) #> [1] NA  1

title: Plot Shannon Entropy Trajectories — plot_entropy_trajectories
code: # Three-period synthetic dataset with distinct trajectory shapes: # Site 1: up-then-down (peak in P2). # Site 2: monotone up. # Per-partition Shannon entropy (bits): #   P1 (Jan)  s1: 0.469  s2: 0.469 #   P2 (Feb)  s1: 1.522  s2: 0.881 #   P3 (Mar)  s1: 0.722  s2: 1.522 df <- data.frame(   s1 = c(     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L),         # P1: 9:1     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L),         # P2: 4:4:2     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L)          # P3: 8:2   ),   s2 = c(     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L),         # P1: 9:1     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L),         # P2: 7:3     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L)          # P3: 4:4:2   ),   Date = rep(     seq(as.Date("2020-01-01"), by = "month", length.out = 3L),     each = 10L   ) )  part_data <- partition_time_windows(   data          = df,   n_sites       = 2L,   window_length = 1L,   window_type   = 3L,   start_date    = "2020-01-01",   end_date      = "2020-04-01",   removez       = FALSE,   removesngl    = FALSE )  result <- plot_entropy_trajectories(part_data = part_data) print(result$Plot)

title: Plot GMM Entropy Class Trajectory for a Single Site — plot_site_class_trajectory
text: function operates $Data_Frame element returned plot_entropy_trajectories, contains columns sites, entropies, class, max_class, period, coverage. Class label interpretation.  green label (upper) records GMM class assigned site partition; red label (lower) records max_class — label highest-entropy component partition.  two labels equal site entered highest-entropy class. title: Plot GMM Entropy Class Trajectory for a Single Site — plot_site_class_trajectory
code: # Three-period synthetic dataset (independent of plot_entropy_trajectories' # example). Three sites with different entropy trajectories; site 1 features # a monotone-up trajectory and progressively higher rank among sites, # producing class changes across all three partitions. # # Site 1 follows a monotone-up trajectory (0.469 -> 0.881 -> 1.522),  # climbing from lowest rank in P1 to highest in P3 for a clear  # "emerging variant" class progression. Site 2 follows the # opposite trajectory (1.522 -> 1.000 -> 0.881), starting as the dominant # high-entropy site and declining as site 1 rises. Site 3 (featured)  # peaks at P2 (0.881 -> 1.522 -> 1.000). # # Per-partition Shannon entropy (bits): #          P1     P2     P3 #   s1   0.469  0.881  1.522    #   s2   1.522  1.000  0.881 #   s3   0.881  1.522  1.000   <- featured site df_cls <- data.frame(   s1 = c(     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L),       # P1: 9:1     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L),       # P2: 7:3     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L)        # P3: 4:4:2   ),   s2 = c(     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L),       # P1: 4:4:2     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L),       # P2: 5:5     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L)        # P3: 7:3   ),   s3 = c(     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L),       # P1: 7:3     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L),       # P2: 4:4:2     c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L, 2L)        # P3: 5:5   ),   Date = rep(     seq(as.Date("2020-01-01"), by = "month", length.out = 3L),     each = 10L   ) )  part_data <- partition_time_windows(   data          = df_cls,   n_sites       = 3L,   window_length = 1L,   window_type   = 3L,   start_date    = "2020-01-01",   end_date      = "2020-04-01",   removez       = FALSE,   removesngl    = FALSE )  # Apply class relabeling so class 1 = highest mean entropy. # plot_entropy_trajectories does not relabel internally; the consumer does. for (i in seq_along(part_data$Clusters)) {   part_data$Clusters[[i]]$DataFrame <-     relabel_entropy_classes(part_data$Clusters[[i]]$DataFrame) }  traj <- plot_entropy_trajectories(part_data)  p <- plot_site_class_trajectory(   data_frame = traj$Data_Frame,   site       = 3L,   site_color = traj$Colors["1"],   xbreaks    = traj$XBreaks,   xlabels    = traj$XLabels ) print(p)

title: Relabel Entropy Classes — relabel_entropy_classes
text: df data frame containing clustering results. Must columns class (integer cluster labels cluster_sites_by_entropy) entropies (numeric Shannon entropy values per row).

title: Relabel Entropy Classes — relabel_entropy_classes
text: data frame relabeled class column (integer) added num_classes column reporting count distinct labels present relabeling.

title: Relabel Entropy Classes — relabel_entropy_classes
text: univariate input (package's typical use case), Mclust orders cluster components increasing mean. Applied per-site Shannon entropy values, places highest-entropy group highest label number (label G G fitted components). function flips convention label 1 always denotes highest-entropy group, natural downstream filtering ("class 1 = top") visual labeling. Cluster identities means unchanged; integer labels remapped. Sentinel preservation. class label 999L marks undifferentiated groups (entropies equal cluster_sites_by_entropy) never relabeled. data frame contains mixture real GMM labels one 999 entries, real labels ranked overwritten; 999 rows pass unchanged. -op return paths. input returned unchanged (num_classes column added) four situations: Missing required columns (class entropies) — warns returns input. Zero rows. rows already carry sentinel 999L. one class label present (relabeling -op).

title: Relabel Entropy Classes — relabel_entropy_classes
code: # Standard case: three classes, ranked by mean entropy. df <- data.frame(   sites     = 1:6,   entropies = c(0.1, 0.1, 0.5, 0.5, 1.2, 1.3),   class     = c(1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L) ) relabel_entropy_classes(df) #>   sites entropies class num_classes #> 1     1       0.1     3           3 #> 2     2       0.1     3           3 #> 3     3       0.5     2           3 #> 4     4       0.5     2           3 #> 5     5       1.2     1           3 #> 6     6       1.3     1           3 # Class 3 (highest mean entropy 1.25) -> 1; class 2 (0.5) -> 2; # class 1 (0.1) -> 3.  # Sentinel preservation: 999 rows pass through unchanged even when # mixed with real classes. df_mixed <- data.frame(   sites     = 1:4,   entropies = c(0.5, 1.2, 0.3, 0.4),   class     = c(1L, 2L, 999L, 1L) ) relabel_entropy_classes(df_mixed) #>   sites entropies class num_classes #> 1     1       0.5     2           3 #> 2     2       1.2     1           3 #> 3     3       0.3   999           3 #> 4     4       0.4     2           3 # Class 2 (highest) -> 1; class 1 -> 2; class 999 stays 999.

text: object available automatically library(ViralEntropR) via lazy loading. produced get_variants curated Excel workbook SARS_CoV_2_VOC_VOI.xlsx (bundled package; see data-raw/sarscov2_variants.R reproducible build script). List elements: WHO_Label List 12. variant label strings (e.g. "Alpha", "Omicron"). Pango_Lineage List 12. Pango lineage designations (e.g. "B.1.1.7"). GISAID_Clade Character(12). GISAID clade strings (e.g. "GRY", "GR/484A"). Nextstrain_Clade Character(12). Nextstrain clade strings (e.g. "20I/501Y.V1"). Country_First_Detected List 12. Country first documented detection per variant. Date_Earliest_Sample Character(12). Month-Year earliest documented sample (e.g. "Sep-2020"). Date_First_Detected Character(12). Month-Year world-level first detection. Date_First_Detected_US Character(12). Month-Year first US detection. Spike_Mutations List 12. Character vectors Spike protein mutations per variant, read Excel workbook. Mutation_Sites List 12. Integer vectors amino acid positions extracted Spike_Mutations. Defining_SNPs List 12. Canonical defining SNP strings (NA variants characterised reference set). Defining_SNP_Sites List 12. Integer positions extracted Defining_SNPs. References Named list three elements: $data – data frame 21 verified references (columns: ID, Authors, Year, Title, Journal_Source, Volume_Issue_Pages, DOI, URL, Type, Variants_Covered, Data_Field, Citation); $display(variant = NULL) – renders interactive datatable, optionally filtered label (requires DT package); $cite(variant) – returns formatted citation strings given label, suitable manuscript use.

title: Simulate Viral Variant Evolution — simulate_variant_evolution
text: Generates synthetic, ground-truth-traceable time series viral amino-acid sequences reference strain progressively challenged emerging variants stochastic growth pairwise competition. Designed controllable signal generator benchmarking entropy, Hellinger, change-point components ViralEntropR pipeline; dynamics deliberately simplified rather faithful biological model evolutionary fitness.

title: Simulate Viral Variant Evolution — simulate_variant_evolution
code: simulate_variant_evolution(   ref_sequences,   n_ref_months = 3L,   start_date,   end_date,   variants_config,   variant_intervals,   n_new_mutations = 1L,   mutation_rate = 2,   mutation_rate_variability = 0.25,   deleterious_rate = 0,   n_deleterious_limit = 1L,   n_sequences_total = 140L,   ref_variability = FALSE,   n_ref_sequences = 100L,   prob_deletion_event = 0,   n_rows_to_delete = 0L,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate Viral Variant Evolution — simulate_variant_evolution
text: ref_sequences Either single character string giving reference amino-acid sequence, data.frame Date column site columns named integer position labels ("1", "2", ..., .character(L); one column per site). sequence length L must least 3, since variant mutations random substitution events sampled sites \(>2\) (sites 1 2 reserved reference variability ref_variability = TRUE). n_ref_months Integer. Duration initial reference phase (months). Default 3. start_date Character Date. Simulation start. end_date Character Date. Simulation end (inclusive month). variants_config Integer vector. Number mutations per variant, e.g., c(2, 6). variant_intervals Integer vector. Months consecutive variant emergences (length length(variants_config) - 1). n_new_mutations Integer. Number de-novo sequence copies introduced variant's first appearance. Despite name, controls count seeded sequences, number mutations carried variant (set per-variant variants_config). Default 1. mutation_rate Numeric scalar vector. Monthly growth multiplier per variant. Default 2. mutation_rate_variability Numeric [0, 1). Fractional spread around mutation_rate: growth drawn Uniform(mult*(1-v), mult*(1+v)). Set 0 deterministic growth. Default 0.25. deleterious_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-mutation probability flagged "deleterious". Default 0. n_deleterious_limit Integer. Per-site cap number variant rows allowed retain flagged ("deleterious") allele given month; overflow reverted reference allele flagged site . See Capped "Deleterious" Sites Details. Default 1. n_sequences_total Integer. Total sequences generated full mutation phase (spread evenly per month). Default 140. ref_variability Logical. TRUE, introduces low-level variability sites 1 2 reference pool. Requires n_ref_sequences >= 6 * n_ref_months variability rows correctly captured multi-variant fill pool; smaller values trigger warning. Default FALSE. n_ref_sequences Integer. ref_sequences single string, number reference rows create. Default 100. prob_deletion_event Numeric [0, 1]. Per-month probability triggering substitution event affecting n_rows_to_delete rows. (Parameter name retained backward compatibility; operation substitution, literal deletion. See Details.) Default 0. n_rows_to_delete Integer. Number rows affected substitution event fires (see prob_deletion_event). Default 0. seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Simulate Viral Variant Evolution — simulate_variant_evolution
text: object class "viralSim", named list: Simulation_Output Data frame sequences, one column per site (named "1", ..., .character(L)) plus Variant (per-row strain label), Phase ("R" "RV" reference rows depending whether row matches wild-type sequence; "M1", "M2", ... mutation-phase months — note "Mn" month label applies every row month regardless strain), Date, Period (1-indexed month), Delet ("Yes" rows affected substitution events, "" otherwise). Variant_Details List per-variant metadata. element em (emergence-month index), pos (mutated site indices), flags (logical vector "deleterious" flags per mutated site), vseq (full mutated reference sequence character vector), mult (growth multiplier), last (count recent month), cum (cumulative count), repl (logical flag set variant displaced successor). Simulation_Dates Date vector simulated months. Baseline_Ref_Sequence Character. wild-type reference string. Delet_Records Named list substitution events keyed date ("YYYY-MM-DD"). entry holds period, date, site, old_aa, new_aa, rows (absolute row indices Simulation_Output modified). Pool Fill-pool data frame final multi-variant competition period (includes ._weight column); NULL one variant ever active.

title: Simulate Viral Variant Evolution — simulate_variant_evolution
text: Simulates trajectory viral population discrete monthly time steps. stochastic components reproducible fixed seed designed expose known emergence pattern downstream detection methods can evaluated . Simulation Phases: Reference Phase: stable period reference strain exists. Reference months resampled replacement assemble n_ref_months monthly batches. Variant Emergence: New variants introduced monthly intervals specified variant_intervals. Pairwise Growth: month newest variant grows alongside immediate predecessor. grow stochastically: variant's next-month count previous-month count times Uniform(mult*(1-variability), mult*(1+variability)). two coupled shared frequency constraint — hard monthly quota ceiling(n_sequences_total / (n_periods - n_ref_months)) trims overshoot. Older, non-paired variants enter fill pool sampling weights 2^(+1). Capped "Deleterious" Sites: Bernoulli-random subset variant's mutated positions flagged probability deleterious_rate. post-emergence month count variant rows still carrying flagged allele capped n_deleterious_limit reverting overflow sequences reference allele flagged site . per-site cap, genotype-level fitness penalty. Random Substitution Events: month independently fires Bernoulli coin flip probability prob_deletion_event. fires, last n_rows_to_delete rows month's batch receive single non-reference amino-acid substitution randomly chosen site (sites 1 2 excluded), rows flagged Delet = "Yes". Parameter names retain "deletion" / "delete" backward compatibility; operation substitution.

title: Tabulate Site Frequency Evolution — tabulate_site_evolution

title: Tabulate Site Frequency Evolution — tabulate_site_evolution
code: tabulate_site_evolution(   partitions,   site_index,   labels = NULL,   alphabet_size = 25L,   zeros = TRUE,   use_letters = TRUE,   relative = FALSE,   digits = 2L,   col_width = "100px",   highlight_col = NULL,   background = "#f0f8ff",   wrap_length = 10L,   save = FALSE,   save_extension = ".html",   save_path = NULL,   return_table = TRUE )

title: Tabulate Site Frequency Evolution — tabulate_site_evolution
text: partitions list data frames, typically produced partition_time_windows. data frame must contain integer-encoded amino acid sequences columns (values 1 alphabet_size). site_index Integer. column index (site) analyse. labels Character vector. Column labels partition. Defaults names(partitions), "P1", "P2", ... unnamed. alphabet_size Integer. Total number possible amino acid codes. Must match encoding used integer encoding (encode_aa_sequence produces values 1-25 default). Default 25L. zeros Logical. TRUE (default), fills missing counts 0. FALSE, replaces zeros ""; see Details type-coercion implication. use_letters Logical. TRUE (default), uses decoded single-character codes 25-symbol alphabet (, R, N, D, C, ..., V, B, Z, X, *, -) row names. FALSE, uses numeric codes 1 alphabet_size. relative Logical. TRUE, converts counts proportions (column-wise division partition size). Default FALSE. digits Integer. Decimal places rounding relative = TRUE. Default 2L. col_width Character. CSS width string applied columns (e.g. "100px"). Default "100px". highlight_col Integer NULL. 1-based column index (relative data columns, counting row-name column) partition highlight background. --range values trigger warning highlight applied. NULL (default) means highlight. background Character. CSS background colour highlighted column. Default "#f0f8ff" (light blue). wrap_length Integer. Character width wrap long column labels using HTML line breaks. Default 10L. save Logical. TRUE, saves rendered HTML table disk via save_kable. Default FALSE. save_extension Character. File extension saved file (including leading dot). Default ".html". save_path Character NULL.  Directory save file.  ...  Must supplied save = TRUE.  Default NULL. return_table Logical. TRUE (default), returns named list raw data frame styled kable object. FALSE, returns styled kable.

title: Tabulate Site Frequency Evolution — tabulate_site_evolution
text: return_table = TRUE, named list: table raw count (proportion) data frame, row names corresponding amino acid codes column names corresponding partition labels. styled kableExtra HTML kable object. return_table = FALSE, returns styled kable object.

title: Tabulate Site Frequency Evolution — tabulate_site_evolution
text: Aggregates amino acid counts per partition using get_site_counts, optionally converts relative frequencies, applies kableExtra styling (column width, column highlighting, striped rows), optionally saves disk. Row names decoded amino acid codes via decode_aa_sequence use_letters = TRUE. Empty partitions. Partitions containing observations contribute -zero columns. relative = TRUE, division zero avoided treating empty-column sums 1, leaving proportions zero. Inspect partition sizes via sapply(partitions, nrow) interpreting table. Note zeros = FALSE. Setting zeros = FALSE replaces numeric zeros empty strings ("") visual clarity kable. conversion forces underlying data frame character storage; numeric operations (sum, mean, etc.) work returned table element. Use zeros = TRUE (default) downstream numerical use intended.
